importlib.metadata/PackageMetadata.name: PackageMetadata
importlib.metadata/PackageMetadata.description: A concrete implementation of the PackageMetadata protocol. In addition to providing the defined protocol methods and attributes, subscripting the instance is equivalent to calling the get() method.

info.excerpt: A concrete implementation of the PackageMetadata protocol. In addition to providing the defined protocol methods and attributes, subscripting the instance is equivalent to calling the get() method.

importlib.metadata/PackagePath.name: PackagePath
importlib.metadata/PackagePath.description: A pathlib.PurePath derived object with additional dist, size, and hash properties corresponding to the distribution package’s installation metadata for that file.

info.excerpt: A pathlib.PurePath derived object with additional dist, size, and hash properties corresponding to the distribution package’s installation metadata for that file.

importlib.metadata/PackageNotFoundError.name: PackageNotFoundError
importlib.metadata/PackageNotFoundError.description: Subclass of ModuleNotFoundError raised by several functions in this module when queried for a distribution package which is not installed in the current Python environment.

info.excerpt: Subclass of ModuleNotFoundError raised by several functions in this module when queried for a distribution package which is not installed in the current Python environment.

importlib.metadata/packages_distribution.name: packages_distributions
importlib.metadata/packages_distribution.description: Return a mapping from the top level module and import package names found via sys.meta_path to the names of the distribution packages (if any) that provide the corresponding files. To allow for namespace packages (which may have members provided by multiple distribution packages), each top level import name maps to a list of distribution names rather than mapping directly to a single name.
